page: Microsoft Azure E8-2s_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the ESv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz, configured with 1 physical core and 2 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The memory configuration is highly dense, providing 64.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 32.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 131 GB, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this server is constrained in processing power and memory throughput relative to other hardware classes. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is rated as weak at 1238.89, while the multi-core score is 1625.89. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with read-only large block transfers at 13.94 GB/sec and write large block transfers at 10.99 GB/sec. Database and web serving capabilities are similarly low, with Passmark database operations scoring 639.86 and static web serving throughput reaching 1.79 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the E8-2s_v3 is its extreme resource asymmetry, pairing a very large memory footprint of 64.0 GB with only 2 vCPUs and low overall compute performance. This makes it a highly specialized, cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require significant CPU processing power. It is best suited for small, memory-heavy databases, caching layers like Redis, or lightweight enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM but minimal parallel processing.
description: The Microsoft Azure E8-2s_v3 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the ESv3 family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz with 1 physical core and 2 vCPUs. The server is characterized by an asymmetric resource profile, offering a substantial 64.0 GB of RAM alongside 131 GB of local storage, with no integrated GPUs. Benchmark results show constrained compute and memory throughput, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1625.89 and uncached memory read bandwidth of 13.94 GB/sec. This hardware configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high memory density but minimal processing power. It is best suited for memory-intensive, low-compute applications such as small databases, caching layers, and lightweight enterprise services.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-2s_v3 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 2 vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 131 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with low compute requirements.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-2s_v3 is an x86_64 server with 2 vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 131 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: An asymmetric memory-optimized virtual server pairing a dense sixty-four gigabyte RAM footprint with dual virtual cores for memory-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
High memory density featuring 64.0 GB of RAM
Includes 131 GB of local storage and Premium Storage capability
Best suited for caching layers, small databases, and memory-heavy enterprise tasks
